required_operator_inputs: make the first backend-recovery slice truthful by landing real multi-row Shape support instead of another editor-only or plan-only pass
keep the mapped advanced-color inventory fixed and only widen the Shape lane runtime authority in this slice
preserve single-row backward compatibility for legacy live tuples and persisted state while making supported two-row Shape lanes real
follow strict TDD: add the focused RED first, then land the minimal runtime owner and live-bridge fix
forbidden_operator_prompts: claim stacked Shape support while the live bridge still rejects more than one enabled Shape row
leave the new Shape stack out of defaults, reset, or diagnostics persistence after claiming the slice landed
widen Grading, Blend, Mask/Domain, or the common-fractal queue in the same slice
reopen the advanced-color inventory definition instead of treating the checked-in mapping as authority

required_acceptance_assertions.description: The advanced color library foundation Phase 5 shape-stack runtime contract validates successfully
The advanced color foundation and shape-stack recovery plans stay synchronized during the slice
